items.title: The schema handler merges schema_json into the draft's existing seo_data
items.precondition: Run the Slice 1b tests with PageModel and the recommendation model mocked.
steps: Execute an approved page_seo_schema recommendation against a draft whose seo_data already carries meta_title.
Inspect the seo_data written to the draft.
items.expected: schema_json is written as a non-empty array and the pre-existing seo_data keys survive (no clobber).
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-write.test.ts passed in the focused 15-test run and the 496-test full suite (Node 22).
items.notes: The write lands on the batch's pinned draft, which the batch auto-publishes as one reversible page version.

items.title: The honesty gate blocks a rank/placement claim before any write
items.precondition: Run the Slice 1b tests with a schema whose description carries a ranking promise.
steps: Execute the recommendation with a rank-claiming description.
Inspect the recommendation status and whether the page was written.
items.expected: The recommendation is failed with the blocked reasons and PageModel.updateSeoDataById is never called.
items.notes: Only descriptive keys are scanned; identity fields (name, alternateName) are excluded so a real practice name is not false-blocked.

items.title: A non-array schema_json fails the recommendation and writes nothing
items.precondition: Run the Slice 1b tests with a bare object and with an empty array.
steps: Execute the recommendation with schema_json as a bare object.
Execute it with an empty array.
items.expected: Both fail the recommendation with a typed reason and perform no write.
items.notes: The whole codebase reads seo_data.schema_json as an array; a bare object would crash the admin SEO score.

items.title: The row builder produces a PENDING, human-gated recommendation of the new type
items.precondition: Build a recommendation row from an owner-approved schema array.
steps: Build the row.
Inspect its target_type, target_meta, and status.
items.expected: target_type is page_seo_schema, target_meta carries the schema array, and no status is set, so the DB default 'pending' applies and a human must approve before executeBatch runs it.
items.notes: CONTRACT ONLY. queueSeoSchemaRecommendation has no production caller (see T10), so this proves the row shape, not a live capability.

items.title: A failed publish marks the batch FAILED instead of completing it
items.precondition: Run executeBatch with publishPage mocked to return a typed error, and again to throw.
steps: Execute a batch whose approved recommendation writes to a page draft.
Make publishPage return an error object on one run and reject on the other.
Inspect the terminal batch status and summary.
items.expected: The batch terminates 'failed' (not 'completed') on both the returned-error and thrown-error paths, and the summary names the unpublished path and the reason.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/ai-command-publish-failure.test.ts passed 3/3. Verified non-vacuous: against the pre-fix code the two failure cases fail with "expected 'completed' to be 'failed'".
items.notes: Article 3.2. The previous loop logged the publishPage error via logger.warn and continued to 'completed'.

items.title: Recommendations whose page did not publish are downgraded from executed to failed
items.precondition: Run executeBatch with a page_seo_schema recommendation and a failing publishPage.
steps: Execute the batch.
Inspect the recommendation's status and stored execution_result.
items.expected: The recommendation is failed with published:false and a reason naming the unpublished draft, so batch stats never claim a change is live while it sits on a draft.
items.notes: Scoped to page_section and page_seo_schema, the types whose write lands on a batch-published draft.

items.title: A fully successful batch still completes and downgrades nothing
steps: Execute the batch with every page publishing.
Inspect the batch status and recommendation updates.
items.expected: The batch completes with the unmodified summary and no recommendation is downgraded, so the fix does not fail healthy batches.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/ai-command-publish-failure.test.ts passed the happy-path case (it also passes against pre-fix code, as expected for a no-regression guard).

items.title: The approved schema is confirmed live before it counts as a success
items.precondition: Run verifyBatchEdits over an executed page_seo_schema recommendation.
steps: Re-read the published page's seo_data.schema_json.
Compare it against the approved array.
items.expected: When the published schema contains the approved entry the recommendation verifies; extra published keys are tolerated and array order is ignored.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-write.test.ts passed the containment-helper and verify cases.
items.notes: page_seo_schema was previously excluded from VERIFIABLE_TARGET_TYPES entirely, so the write was never checked against the live page.

items.title: A schema write that did not reach the live page is failed, not reported as success
items.precondition: Run verifyBatchEdits against a stale published schema, a missing published row, and an unreadable read.
steps: Publish a page whose schema_json lacks the approved entry.
Remove the published row entirely.
Make the published read reject.
items.expected: All three downgrade the recommendation to failed with a verify_reason; an unreadable live page fails rather than defaulting to success.
items.evidence: src/__tests__/get-found-write.test.ts passed all three downgrade cases.
items.notes: Deliberately stricter than the HTML token heuristic: the approved schema is exact JSON, so presence is decidable. A false failure costs one idempotent re-run; a false success would claim structured data is live when it is not.

items.title: NOT PROVEN: no production path creates a page_seo_schema recommendation
steps: Grep for callers of queueSeoSchemaRecommendation.
Grep for callers of the upstream Slice 1a runGetFoundChecker.
items.expected: A production caller turns a get-found analysis result into a pending recommendation.
items.evidence: Both are referenced only by their own definition and their tests. queueSeoSchemaRecommendation: service.get-found-write.ts plus get-found-write.test.ts only. runGetFoundChecker: getFoundChecker.ts:88 plus get-found-checker.test.ts only.
items.notes: The producer is blocked upstream: Slice 1a's checker is itself not invoked from any application path, so there is no get-found analysis result in production to wire from. Building one needs the deferred pieces (hosted-page fetch, a GBP identity source, a get-found batch type and its UI). The execute half IS production-wired: executeBatch dispatches page_seo_schema to the handler, reachable from POST /:id/ai-command/:batchId/execute.
items.waiver: Contract narrowed instead of faked. service.get-found-write.ts now states that Slice 1b ships the execute half plus the row contract, that queueSeoSchemaRecommendation has no production caller, and that the type is inert until a producer lands. The reviewer explicitly allowed 'add a real production path, OR honestly narrow the stated contract'; this is the second option, recorded here so the gap stays visible rather than reading as shipped.
